LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SUNNYSIDE COGENERATION PARTNERS, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THIS DISPUTE IS GOVERNED BY COMMERICIAL CODE SECTION 2306 BECAUSE THE P.P.A IS NOT BASED ON THE ACTUAL REQUIREMENTS OF P.G & E. GIVEN THAT P.G.& E. PAID FOR THE RIGHT TO DEMAND FULL CAPACITY AND SUNNYSIDE ELECTED TO BE PAID ON THAT BASIS, TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ARE PRESENTED AS TO P.G.& E.'S ALLEGED BREACH AND WHETHER THE PARTIES' CONDUCT, IN FACT, CONSTITUTED A MODIFICATION OF THE P.P.A. FULL CAPACITY REQUIREMENT. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Off Calendar Jul-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-07-463358 WITH CASE CGC-06-452312.
APPEARANCES: WILLIAM F. MURPHY, ESQUIRE AND WILLIAM WILSON, ESQUIRE (DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, 415.397.2700) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF SUNNYSIDE COGENERATION. LAURIE EDELSTEIN, ESQUIRE AND RANDALL KIM, ESQUIRE (BRUNE & RICHARD, 415.563.0600) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT P.G. & E.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR THE REASONS STATED IN CONNECTION WITH DEMURRER IN CASE #07-463358. P.G.& E. FAILED TO PLEAD ITS COMPULSORY COUNTER-CLAIM IN THIS ACTION. MR. MURPHY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/KD) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SUNNYSIDE COGENERATION PARTNERS, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: WILLIAM F. MURPHY, ESQUIRE AND WILLIAM WILSON, ESQUIRE (DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, 415.397.2700) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF SUNNYSIDE COGENERATION. LAURIE EDELSTEIN, ESQUIRE AND RANDALL KIM, ESQUIRE (BRUNE & RICHARD, 415.563.0600) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT P.G. & E.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THIS DISPUTE IS GOVERNED BY COMMERICIAL CODE SECTION 2306 BECAUSE THE P.P.A IS NOT BASED ON THE ACTUAL REQUIREMENTS OF P.G & E. GIVEN THAT P.G.& E. PAID FOR THE RIGHT TO DEMAND FULL CAPACITY AND SUNNYSIDE ELECTED TO BE PAID ON THAT BASIS, TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ARE PRESENTED AS TO P.G.& E.'S ALLEGED BREACH AND WHETHER THE PARTIES' CONDUCT, IN FACT, CONSTITUTED A MODIFICATION OF THE P.P.A. FULL CAPACITY REQUIREMENT. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/kd) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
